title: Branch Manager
short_description: A government has three branches — one makes the laws, one carries them out, one decides what's fair. Sort each job into the right branch.
description: A government has three teams called branches, and each has a different job: one MAKES the laws, one CARRIES THEM OUT, and one DECIDES what's fair. Read each job and sort it into the right branch — Lawmakers (legislative), Leaders (executive), or Judges (judicial). It teaches the non-partisan idea of separated powers and why it keeps government balanced. Grade bands ramp from the big idea in kid-friendly words, to trickier jobs, to real government terms like Congress, Supreme Court and signing a bill into law. A free online civics and government game for kids in grades 2–5, with streak bonuses feeding your score and GP.
controls: Mouse / tap the branch of government each job belongs to.
